From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2024 studio album by STAYC

| Metamorphic | |

| Length | 39:29 |
| Language | Korean |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | * [High Up](/wiki/High_Up_Entertainment "High Up Entertainment") * [Kakao](/wiki/Kakao_Entertainment "Kakao Entertainment") |
| [STAYC](/wiki/STAYC "STAYC") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[Teenfresh](/wiki/Teenfresh "Teenfresh")* (2023) | ***Metamorphic*** (2024) | *[...l](/wiki/...l "...l")* (2024) | | |
|  | |
| [Singles](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") from  *Metamorphic* | |
| 1. "[Cheeky Icy Thang](/wiki/Cheeky_Icy_Thang "Cheeky Icy Thang")" Released: July 1, 2024 | |
|  | |

***Metamorphic*** is the first [studio album](/wiki/Studio_album "Studio album") by South Korean girl group [STAYC](/wiki/STAYC "STAYC"). It was released by [High Up Entertainment](/wiki/High_Up_Entertainment "High Up Entertainment") on July 1, 2024, and contains 14 tracks, including the lead single "[Cheeky Icy Thang](/wiki/Cheeky_Icy_Thang "Cheeky Icy Thang")".

## Background and release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metamorphic_(album)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background and release")]

On June 3, 2024, High Up Entertainment announced that STAYC would be releasing their first studio album titled *Metamorphic* on July 1.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Eight days later, the trailer film was released.[[2]](#cite_note-2) On June 16, the track listing was released with "Cheeky Icy Thang" announced as the lead single.[[3]](#cite_note-3) On June 23, the first music video teaser for "Cheeky Icy Thang" was released.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The highlight medley teaser videos was released on June 24 and 25.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The album was released alongside the music video for "Cheeky Icy Thang" on July 1.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Promotion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metamorphic_(album)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Promotion")]

Prior to the release of *Metamorphic*, on July 1, 2024, STAYC held a live event aimed at introducing the album and connecting with their fanbase.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Additionally, to commemorate the release of the album, they opened a [pop-up store](/wiki/Pop-up_store "Pop-up store") event in Seoul, South Korea, running from June 28 to July 21.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Critical reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metamorphic_(album)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Critical reception")]

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| *[IZM](/wiki/IZM "IZM")*[[9]](#cite_note-izm-9) | StarStar |
| *[NME](/wiki/NME "NME")*[[10]](#cite_note-nme-10) | StarStarStarStar |

*Metamorphic* received mixed-to-positive reviews from the [music critics](/wiki/Journalist "Journalist"). Gladys Yeo of *[NME](/wiki/NME "NME")* praised the album for showing the girl group's musical range, writing that STAYC "pushed themselves to their limits, exploring a myriad of new soundscapes and subject matter."[[10]](#cite_note-nme-10) Han Sung-hyun, in a mixed review writing for *[IZM](/wiki/IZM "IZM")*, described it as a "passive collection of attempts that lack the symbolism of a first full-length album."[[9]](#cite_note-izm-9)
